days.summary: Day one, from the initial hello-world Worker to a working deep-research assistant: a streaming chatbot moved off the Claude API onto Berget with Exa web search, a model dropdown backed by Berget's catalog, and the research flow rebuilt as an orchestrated pipeline with validation. The UI took shape alongside it — PWA support with the flag-swirl icon, the sky-blue theme, default Markdown rendering, image attachments for vision models, and the time-budget slider.

days.summary: Accounts and access landed: Google-only sign-in with auto-provisioning, an admin-approval gate, the D1 database, the four-window quota model, and the admin interface. Answer recovery and SSE hardening kept dropped connections from losing a finished answer, while the UI moved to the floating frosted-glass header and one-pane composer — plus the PDF report, document attachments, the /help and build-story pages, and the MIT license.

days.summary: Storage and evaluation matured: opt-in R2/Vectorize cloud storage with client-side document RAG and encrypted local chat history, EXIF-GPS reverse geocoding and attachment-metadata extraction, and the spiderweb web-search knob. The model-matrix eval harness arrived and drove several rounds of pipeline fixes (fetch timeouts, source-diversity caps, the exceededCpu/Workers-Paid CPU limit), alongside a message/notification center and the first unit-test suite.

days.summary: Location intelligence and projects arrived: the Google Maps enrichment (Places, Street View, Static Maps) with a vision-describe helper, and projects as RAG-indexed collections of chats and files. Split model routing pinned the JSON planning phases to a fixed reliable model, and the day also added the Shodan integration, the incognito ghost toggle, a composer camera button, cross-request Exa caching, and encryption of attached-file originals at rest.

days.summary: The busiest day (77 commits): the multi-round iOS history-pane swipe saga was finally cracked (h6–h17, the on-device-trace method), and major features shipped — the inline quiz capability, the server-side chat-interaction log with a ghost opt-out, Hugging Face Hub search joining the pipeline, and the site exposed as an MCP server. A security assessment fixed several high-severity findings, scored LLM-judged benchmarks were added, and CLAUDE.md was split into on-demand skills.

days.summary: The Tokemon open-world AR game shipped as the first plug-in of a new games subsystem, and the provider registry grew to three — Anthropic (opus/sonnet/haiku) and OpenAI (GPT) joined Berget. Feedback mode opened a per-reply dialogue with the development loop, the Maps integration gained the clickable image deck and journey view, and equal Swedish/English intent routing became a load-bearing invariant with a parity audit.

days.summary: The client-side Se/cure tier was born: no-account deep research running entirely in the browser on the user's own keys (direct OpenAI/Groq calls, browser-local sealed storage, the secret-keyed vault, and published /cure replays), presented as a khaki look-and-feel twin of the app with the ghost now the door to it. The experimental in-browser Linux execution sandbox and bash-lite agent also debuted, alongside the deepresearch.se/cure–/rver wordmark branding and a broad @ts-check typing sweep.

days.summary: The execution sandbox learned to mount chat and project files into the Linux VM, and Berget joined Se/cure as its third client-side provider. Both tiers gained a settings gear with a one-field, provider-auto-detecting key form; the sync-with-latest-main SessionStart hook was added; and several CheerpX cross-origin-isolation fixes (COEP require-corp for iOS) plus a vault-core split that unbroke /cure for anonymous visitors landed.

days.summary: Introspection/developer mode matured — answering from the site's own deployed source via a committed source-RAG index, the titanium theme and TIN mascot, and native tool-calling over the code — while three admin decision boards (security-risk, features, and the panel-attention board) plus a boards discovery index stood up the priority-driven work loops. A security-fix round closed several findings (P-2/P-3/P-8), and the day also brought the /architecture page, the umbrella intro animations, the CamelCase wordmarks, and this Project pulse dashboard.

days.summary: Introspection mode gained an OWASP Top 10 default with a security-assessment corpus and executive-summary reports, surfaced the skills catalog to every model, and settled its per-tier skins (amethyst Se/rver, ruby Se/cure). The git workflow was reconciled — the merge barrier, both PR and direct-merge styles, and the merge-branches and pr skills with their branch ledger — the mission was reframed around LLM-privacy-capabilities research, and the sandbox picked up composer-focus pre-warming and full boot-timeline debug coverage.
